subtopics.title: Types of Numbers
common_misconceptions: Zero is a natural number
All fractions are rational numbers but not all rationals are fractions
Negative numbers are not real numbers
subtopics.teaching_note: Use number line to illustrate relationships. Emphasize that every natural number is a whole number but not vice versa. Worked example: Classify -3, 0, 1/2, √2, 7 into appropriate number sets.

subtopics.title: Number Bases
common_misconceptions: Confusing place values in different bases
Thinking base 5 only uses digits 1-5 instead of 0-4
subtopics.teaching_note: Start with familiar base 10, then extend to other bases. Worked example: Convert 25₁₀ to base 2. Solution: 25 ÷ 2 = 12 r 1, 12 ÷ 2 = 6 r 0, 6 ÷ 2 = 3 r 0, 3 ÷ 2 = 1 r 1, 1 ÷ 2 = 0 r 1. Read remainders upwards: 11001₂.

subtopics.title: Basic Set Concepts
common_misconceptions: Confusing ∈ (element of) with ⊆ (subset of)
Thinking {0} is the empty set
Not understanding that A ⊆ B includes A = B
subtopics.teaching_note: Use Venn diagrams extensively. Emphasize proper notation: ∈ for elements, ⊆ for subsets.

subtopics.title: Set Operations
common_misconceptions: Confusing union with intersection
Forgetting to subtract intersection in the cardinality formula
Incorrectly shading Venn diagram regions
subtopics.teaching_note: WAEC/JAMB frequently test Venn diagram problems with two or three sets. Worked example: In a class of 40 students, 20 study Math, 25 study English, and 8 study both. How many study neither? Solution: n(M∪E) = 20 + 25 - 8 = 37. Neither = 40 - 37 = 3.

subtopics.title: Expansion and Factorization
common_misconceptions: (a+b)² = a² + b² (forgetting 2ab)
Confusing expansion with factorization
Sign errors when expanding negative brackets
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize the FOIL method for binomials. Worked example: Expand (2x+3)(x-4). Solution: 2x² - 8x + 3x - 12 = 2x² - 5x - 12. Factorize: x² - 5x + 6 = (x-2)(x-3).

subtopics.title: Algebraic Fractions
common_misconceptions: Canceling terms instead of factors
Incorrectly finding common denominators
Forgetting to factor before simplifying
subtopics.teaching_note: Always emphasize factoring numerator and denominator first before simplifying. Worked example: Simplify (x²-9)/(x²+6x+9) = (x-3)(x+3)/(x+3)² = (x-3)/(x+3).

subtopics.title: Solving Quadratic Equations
common_misconceptions: Thinking all quadratics factorize easily
Sign errors in the quadratic formula
Forgetting to set equation to zero first
subtopics.teaching_note: The quadratic formula: x = [-b ± √(b²-4ac)] / 2a. Worked example: Solve 2x² - 7x + 3 = 0. a=2, b=-7, c=3. Discriminant = 49-24 = 25. x = (7±5)/4. x = 3 or x = 1/2.

subtopics.title: Quadratic Functions and Graphs
common_misconceptions: Confusing roots with vertex
Not understanding that a>0 gives minimum, a<0 gives maximum
Incorrectly identifying axis of symmetry
subtopics.teaching_note: Vertex form: y = a(x-h)² + k where (h,k) is the vertex. Axis of symmetry: x = h = -b/2a.

subtopics.title: Laws of Logarithms
common_misconceptions: log(a+b) = log(a) + log(b) - NO!
Confusing log with natural log (ln)
Forgetting log(1) = 0 and log(base) = 1
subtopics.teaching_note: Key laws: log(AB) = logA + logB; log(A/B) = logA - logB; log(Aⁿ) = nlogA. Worked example: If log₂(x) = 5, then x = 2⁵ = 32.

subtopics.title: Logarithmic and Exponential Equations
common_misconceptions: Taking log of both sides incorrectly
Not checking for extraneous solutions
Confusing exponential growth with linear growth
subtopics.teaching_note: Worked example: Solve 2ˣ = 64. Take log: xlog2 = log64, so x = log64/log2 = 6. Or recognize 64 = 2⁶, so x = 6.

subtopics.title: Arithmetic Progression (AP)
common_misconceptions: Confusing the nth term formula with sum formula
Using wrong value for d (common difference)
Off-by-one errors in counting terms
subtopics.teaching_note: Worked example: Find the 20th term of 3, 7, 11, ... Here a=3, d=4. T₂₀ = 3 + 19(4) = 3 + 76 = 79. Sum of first 20 terms: S₂₀ = 20/2(3 + 79) = 10 × 82 = 820.

subtopics.title: Geometric Progression (GP)
common_misconceptions: Using AP formulas for GP problems
Forgetting that |r| must be < 1 for sum to infinity
Confusing r>1 with 0<r<1 cases
subtopics.teaching_note: Worked example: Find the sum to infinity of 8, 4, 2, ... Here a=8, r=1/2. S∞ = 8/(1-1/2) = 8/(1/2) = 16.

subtopics.title: Binomial Expansion
common_misconceptions: ⁿCᵣ = ⁿCₙ₋ᵣ confusion
Off-by-one errors in finding the rth term
Sign errors in (a-b)ⁿ expansions
subtopics.teaching_note: The (r+1)th term is ⁿCᵣ aⁿ⁻ʳ bʳ. Worked example: Find the coefficient of x³ in (2+x)⁵. This is ⁵C₃ × 2² × 1³ = 10 × 4 = 40.

subtopics.title: Angles and Parallel Lines
common_misconceptions: Confusing alternate with corresponding angles
Assuming lines are parallel without proof
Incorrectly applying angle sum properties
subtopics.teaching_note: Use Z, F, C patterns to identify angle relationships. Worked example: Two parallel lines cut by a transversal. If one angle is 70°, find all other angles.

subtopics.title: Polygons
common_misconceptions: Exterior angle sum depends on number of sides - NO, always 360°
Confusing interior and exterior angles
Forgetting that regular means all sides AND angles equal
subtopics.teaching_note: Interior angle sum = (n-2) × 180°. Each interior angle of regular n-gon = (n-2)×180°/n. Exterior angle of regular n-gon = 360°/n.

subtopics.title: Circle Theorems
common_misconceptions: Angle at centre = angle at circumference - NO, it's twice!
Forgetting alternate segment theorem
Confusing cyclic quadrilateral opposite angles with adjacent
subtopics.teaching_note: Key theorem: Angle at centre = 2 × angle at circumference. Cyclic quad: opposite angles sum to 180°. Alternate segment: angle between tangent and chord = angle in alternate segment.

subtopics.title: Trigonometric Ratios
common_misconceptions: sin(30°) = 1/2 but thinking sin(60°) = 1/2 too
Confusing sine and cosine
Using calculator instead of exact values for special angles
subtopics.teaching_note: Special angles: sin30°=1/2, cos30°=√3/2, tan30°=1/√3; sin45°=cos45°=1/√2; sin60°=√3/2, cos60°=1/2. Worked example: Find x if sin(x) = √3/2. x = 60°.

subtopics.title: Applications of Trigonometry
common_misconceptions: Confusing angle of elevation with depression
Incorrectly labeling bearings
Using sine rule when cosine rule is needed
subtopics.teaching_note: Sine rule: a/sinA = b/sinB = c/sinC. Cosine rule: a² = b² + c² - 2bc·cosA. Worked example: In ΔABC, a=5, b=7, C=60°. Find c. Using cosine rule: c² = 25 + 49 - 70(0.5) = 74 - 35 = 39. c = √39.

subtopics.title: Data Representation
common_misconceptions: Confusing histogram with bar chart
Incorrectly calculating angles for pie charts
Not using class boundaries for histograms
subtopics.teaching_note: Pie chart angle = (frequency/total) × 360°. For histograms, use class boundaries and frequency density = frequency/class width.

subtopics.title: Measures of Central Tendency
common_misconceptions: Adding frequencies and dividing by number of classes for grouped mean
Confusing modal class with mode
Not ordering data before finding median
subtopics.teaching_note: Grouped mean: Σfx/Σf where x is midpoint. Assumed mean: A + Σfd/Σf where d = x - A. Median from ogive: find (n/2)th value on cumulative frequency curve.

subtopics.title: Measures of Dispersion
common_misconceptions: Confusing variance with standard deviation
Forgetting to take square root for standard deviation
Using wrong formula for grouped data
subtopics.teaching_note: Standard deviation = √variance. Worked example: Find SD of 2, 4, 6, 8, 10. Mean = 6. Deviations: -4, -2, 0, 2, 4. Squared: 16, 4, 0, 4, 16. Variance = 40/5 = 8. SD = √8 = 2√2.

subtopics.title: Basic Probability Concepts
common_misconceptions: Probability can be greater than 1
Equally likely assumption when not stated
Confusing mutually exclusive with independent events
subtopics.teaching_note: P(E) = number of favorable outcomes / total outcomes. Always 0 ≤ P(E) ≤ 1. P(E') = 1 - P(E).

subtopics.title: Addition and Multiplication Rules
common_misconceptions: Always adding probabilities instead of multiplying for 'and'
Not checking if events are independent
Confusing P(A|B) with P(B|A)
subtopics.teaching_note: AND = multiply (for independent), OR = add (subtract intersection if not mutually exclusive). Worked example: P(A) = 0.4, P(B) = 0.5, P(A∩B) = 0.2. P(A∪B) = 0.4 + 0.5 - 0.2 = 0.7.

subtopics.title: Differentiation
common_misconceptions: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ instead of nxⁿ⁻¹
Forgetting to reduce power by 1
Confusing derivative with function value
subtopics.teaching_note: Power rule: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹. d/dx(constant) = 0. Worked example: If y = 3x⁴ - 2x² + 5x - 7, find dy/dx. dy/dx = 12x³ - 4x + 5. At x=1, gradient = 12 - 4 + 5 = 13.

subtopics.title: Integration
common_misconceptions: Forgetting +C for indefinite integrals
Integrating instead of differentiating or vice versa
Not reversing the power rule correctly
subtopics.teaching_note: Integration reverses differentiation. ∫xⁿ dx = xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) + C for n ≠ -1. Definite integral gives area: ∫ₐᵇ f(x)dx = F(b) - F(a). Worked example: ∫(2x + 3)dx = x² + 3x + C.
